---
title: "Sports Analytics in Practice"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| cache: True
#| out.width: "100%"
knitr::include_graphics('image/UoL Zoom Backgrounds_20226.jpg')
```

## Welcome! {.unnumbered}

Welcome to the course website dedicated to the ULMS744 module: *Sports Analytics in Practice*! On this site, you will find the **coding seminar materials**. You can navigate to each week’s material by clicking on the specific sidebar menu link. Please do let me know if you are struggling to access anything. Below you will find information related to the organisational aspects of the course. These are also covered in the intro lecture slides.

## Key Details {.unnumbered}

Shunya Kimura will be taking **Semester 2 Weeks 07 -- 11**.

**Contact**: s.kimura\@liverpool.ac.uk ([book a meeting](https://outlook.office.com/bookwithme/user/7c4dd5b5714749a782d4dba134872023@liverpool.ac.uk?anonymous&ismsaljsauthenabled&ep=plink))

The module is also supported by Ayan Orujob who will be taking over the first six weeks. He offers his own office hours for additional support outside of the lectures/seminars, and he'll let you know when they take place.

Keep an eye on the [module announcements](https://canvas.liverpool.ac.uk/courses/85669/announcements) page for further updates and consult [Canvas](https://canvas.liverpool.ac.uk/courses/85669) for all essential module information, including lecture [slides](https://canvas.liverpool.ac.uk/courses/85669/modules)/[recordings](https://canvas.liverpool.ac.uk/courses/85669/external_tools/2009), reading lists and key details about [assignments](https://canvas.liverpool.ac.uk/courses/85669/assignments) submission etc. **This workbook is designed primarily to support the seminar sessions**, and it contains the key materials, exercises and guidance that we will work through during seminars.

## Timetable {.unnumbered}

Seminars for this module run on the highlighted week below. The topics covered over the next nine weeks are:

| No. | Week Beginning | Topic |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
|  |  | **Reading week** |
| 7 | 9$^{th}$ Mar. | [Getting Started]({{< var urls.u01 >}}) |
| **8** | [16$^{th}$ Mar.]{.mark} | [Integrated Data Publishing with Quarto]({{< var urls.u02 >}}) |
|  |  | **Easter period** |
| **9** | [13$^{th}$ Apr.]{.mark} | [API & Data Sourcing]({{< var urls.u03 >}}) |
| **10** | [20$^{th}$Apr.]{.mark} | [Interactive Dashboards with Shinylive for R]({{< var urls.u04 >}}) |
| 11 | 27$^{th}$ Apr. | [The Professional Portfolio]({{< var urls.u05 >}}) |

## Help & Support {.unnumbered}

For specific assistance with this module, you can:

-   Refer to the [Canvas assignments](https://canvas.liverpool.ac.uk/courses/85669/assignments) tab for queries about module assignments

-   Ask a question at the end of lectures or during the seminars

-   Book into the Office Hours (link to Bookings with me)

## Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

This workbook is created using the [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) publishing system. It takes inspiration and design elements from:

-   The [GEOG0030: Geocomputation](https://jtvandijk.github.io/GEOG0030/00-index.html) workbook by [Justin van Dijk](https://www.mappingdutchman.com)
-   The [POLS0010: Data Analysis 2024](https://jcheshire.com/pols0010-data-analysis-2024/) workbook by [James Cheshire](https://jcheshire.com/)
-   The [GEOG0051: Mining Social and Geographic Datasets 2020](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/module-catalogue/modules/mining-social-and-geographic-datasets-GEOG0051) materials by [Stephen Law](https://profiles.ucl.ac.uk/21695-stephen-law)
-   The [POLS0010: Data Analysis 2019](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/8ryqys1qxtdkiximp9mkh/POLS0010-Course-Outline-2023-24.pdf?rlkey=x1p3r7kwjplf1rokxo5gu5p4e&e=1&dl=0) materials by [Tom O'Grady](https://tomogradypolitics.wordpress.com/)
